Deep learning refers to a subset of machine learning that uses artificial neural networks with multiple layers as an algorithm. It allows machines to learn from and extract features from large datasets.

The transformer is a family of neural network architectures based on multi-head attention mechanisms. Each token in text data is converted into a vector using word embeddings, and contextualized within the context window with other tokens via parallel multi-head attention. Positional information is injected through positional encodings or learned positional embeddings to account for order effects.

Self-attention allows the signal of key tokens to be amplified and less important tokens to be diminished, making it more effective at contextualizing tokens within their context window without being affected by positional encodings.
